contacts.title: Contacts

empty.title: Your first contact list starts here
empty.body: Once you add your first few people, the system will begin showing warmth, rhythm, and next-step signals here.
empty.filteredBody: Nothing is showing for these filters right now. Loosening the filters a bit may reveal the right people.

signalBoard.title: Contact Signals
signalBoard.subtitle: A light decision layer that shows where your attention should go first today.

followUps.title: Stabilize
followUps.body: {{count}} people are waiting on rhythm. Clearing overdue or due-today follow-ups first will steady the board.

warm.title: Advance
warm.body: {{count}} people are in the warm zone. A single clear next step will work better than a longer explanation.

reactivate.title: Reopen
reactivate.body: {{count}} people have gone quiet. A short, low-pressure touch can bring them back into motion.

nextMove.stabilize: Stabilize the list first. {{dueNow}} follow-ups are overdue or due today, so clearing those before chasing warm opportunities will calm the flow.
nextMove.advance: The advancement window is open. With {{warmWindow}} warm people in play, clarifying one next step will work better than pushing for a decision.
nextMove.reactivate: The list has gone a bit quiet. {{reactivationPool}} people are ready for reactivation, so low-pressure check-ins are the best move right now.
nextMove.build: The list looks clean. This is a good moment to build fresh momentum by moving {{freshProspects}} newer contacts with short invites.

contacts.connectionError: Contacts cannot be loaded right now.
contacts.connectionErrorHint: Check your connection and refresh the page to try again.
contacts.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete this contact?
contacts.bulkDeleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete {{count}} contacts?

detail.aiDescription: Based on this person's profile and your communication history, you can get instant guidance from the AI assistant on what kind of message to send or what your next step should be.
detail.aiPlaceholder: AI-powered suggestions will appear here in Phase 4.

follow_up.title: Re-open the conversation softly
follow_up.body: The need here is not more pressure, but visible rhythm. A short reminder and one small question usually works better.
follow_up.objectionHint: Likely hesitation: delay, avoidance, or being busy.
follow_up.pipeline: Now: send a short, low-pressure follow-up

invite.title: Turn first contact into curiosity
invite.body: This is not the moment for a long explanation. A natural opening, a curiosity hook, and a low-pressure invite will land better.
invite.objectionHint: Likely hesitation: time resistance or early defensiveness.
invite.pipeline: Now: open with a short invite or first-touch message

decision.title: Clarify the next step
decision.body: There is interest here, but progress is not fully clear. Instead of pushing for a decision, suggest one next step that lowers friction.
decision.objectionHint: Likely hesitation: budget or decision weight.
decision.pipeline: Now: make the next step clearer and lighter

objection.title: Turn hesitation into dialogue
objection.body: This person may be looking for safety before movement. Lower the pressure first, validate the concern, and leave a small opening.
objection.objectionHint: Likely hesitation: trust, doubt, or needing time to think.
objection.pipeline: Now: soften the objection and keep the dialogue open

onboarding.title: Build the first rhythm together
onboarding.body: At this stage motivation alone is not enough. Short direction, a small target, and early contact help a new person stay in motion.
onboarding.objectionHint: Likely hesitation: low confidence or not knowing what to do next.
onboarding.pipeline: Now: set the first action and rhythm together

interaction.empty:

risk.body: {{count}} people need rhythm. Clearing overdue steps first will steady the board.

momentum.title: Momentum Window
momentum.body: {{count}} people are in a warm advancement zone. One clear next step works better than a longer pitch here.

openings.title: Fresh Openings
openings.body: {{count}} people look ready for a first touch. Short, natural openings will land better here.

nextMove.stabilize: Stabilize the flow first. {{risk}} people carry follow-up risk, so settling those cards before chasing warm opportunities will calm the board.
nextMove.advance: The advancement window is open. With {{momentum}} warm people in play, clarifying one next step is stronger than adding more pressure.
nextMove.open: The board looks clean. This is a good moment to move {{openings}} fresh openings with short invites and natural first messages.
nextMove.nurture: The board is balanced right now. Instead of forcing new pressure, keep the rhythm visible and stay present with the people already in motion.

columnCues.interested: Turn interest into a next step
columnCues.lost: Learn and do not force it

dashboard.welcomeSubtitle: Welcome to your Network Marketing Master dashboard.

onboarding.label: Getting Started
onboarding.title: {{name}}, let’s set the system up together
onboarding.subtitle: You should not have to guess where to begin. Once these four basics are in place, the product starts working much more naturally for you.

profile.title: Clarify your profile
profile.body: Your name and basic identity help the product feel more personal from the start.
profile.hint: As a first step, quickly review the settings area.

first_contact.title: Add your first contact
first_contact.body: The system really starts working once you begin building rhythm with a real person.
first_contact.hint: Do not overthink it. Start with the most real person already on your mind today.

first_follow_up.title: Create your first follow-up
first_follow_up.body: A contact without a next step disappears quickly. A small follow-up changes the whole flow.
first_follow_up.hint: As soon as a follow-up appears on the calendar, the product can guide you much better.

first_learning.title: Open one training resource
first_learning.body: The academy makes objection handling and message flow noticeably smarter.
first_learning.hint: Even one resource can improve the quality of your next messages and follow-ups.

dashboard.noCandidatesAddedThisWeek: No new candidates added this week
dashboard.academyNotesHint: Keep your pace with a short reminder from Academy or the objection bank.
dashboard.noAcademyNotes: No academy note to show yet
dashboard.todayBirthdaysHint: Don't miss the contacts whose birthday is today.
dashboard.noBirthdaysToday: No contacts have a birthday today
dashboard.birthdayEmpty: The birthday message will appear here
dashboard.birthdayAiPrompt: Today is this person's birthday. Generate a short, warm, personal, relationship-focused celebration message that naturally uses the profile details.
dashboard.messageCopiedForChannel: Message copied. You can send it through {{channel}}.

focus.title: Today's Focus Board
focus.subtitle: We turned the operating-system thinking from the reference projects into a simple decision layer powered by your real data.
focus.empty: No critical action stands out right now. You can open the day with fresh reach-outs or academy rhythm.
focus.rhythmSubtitle: See the areas that either slow your distributor flow down or push it forward at a glance.

headlines.opportunities: {{count}} warm opportunities can move before they cool off
headlines.new_reachouts: {{count}} new people are waiting for a first touch

summaries.follow_ups: First clean up overdue and due-today conversations. Returning to warm opportunities after that usually improves the quality of the day.
summaries.opportunities: Today the strongest signal is people with heat and decision potential. A small but clear next step can create momentum.
summaries.new_reachouts: There are people in the lead pool still waiting for a first touch. Low-pressure, short, natural openings usually work best here.

reasons.overdue: The follow-up is overdue. Re-entering before warmth drops matters here.
reasons.today: An action is due today. A small touch without delay usually works well.
reasons.presentation: They are in a presentation or decision window. Clarifying the next step would help.
reasons.opportunity: Interest still looks warm. It is a good moment to move the conversation forward.
reasons.newContact: Recently added. There is still a natural window for the first touch.
reasons.freshTouch: The first contact is still fresh. Building rhythm before silence sets in is an advantage.

warmOpportunities.title: Warm Opportunities
warmOpportunities.hint: People showing interest and close to progress

newReachOuts.title: First Touches
newReachOuts.hint: Recently added people with no rhythm yet

playbooks.follow_ups: Raise clarity, not pressure. Briefly reference the last touch, suggest one next move, and leave the conversation open.
playbooks.opportunities: Do not overload a warm person with detail. Confirm the interest and offer one next step that lowers decision friction.
playbooks.new_reachouts: The goal of a first reach-out is not a sale, but a safe opening. Build a short connection, spark curiosity, and start a natural dialogue.

gamePlan.label: Today's Game Plan
gamePlan.title: Run the day with more clarity
gamePlan.subtitle: See where to look first, what to keep warm next, and how to protect your rhythm in one place.

primary.follow_ups: Clean up overdue and due-today conversations first.
primary.opportunities: Do not leave warm people near a decision point waiting today.
primary.new_reachouts: Open with a short, natural first touch for the people still waiting.

next.follow_ups: After the backlog, move the top {{count}} warm opportunities forward.
next.opportunities: After warm opportunities, return to the {{count}} new people still waiting for rhythm.
next.new_reachouts: After fresh reach-outs, review the {{count}} follow-up actions you still should not leave open.

stability.follow_ups: Use a short check-in, a soft reminder, and one clear next step.
stability.opportunities: Instead of adding detail, offer one next step that makes the decision easier.
stability.new_reachouts: Choose openings that feel like a natural connection, not a sales pitch.

support.title: Field Support
support.subtitle: We pulled the most useful academy and objection support for the rhythm you are in today.
support.coachReason: Turns today’s plan into a usable field tone.

academyReasons.follow_ups: Today the strongest need is better follow-up language and sequence.
academyReasons.opportunities: Today content that lowers decision friction will help most.
academyReasons.new_reachouts: Today first-touch and invitation tone matters more.

objectionReasons.follow_ups: Quiet or delaying conversations often surface this kind of hesitation.
objectionReasons.opportunities: This is one of the most common pressure points when a warm person nears a decision.
objectionReasons.new_reachouts: In early conversations, defensiveness and hesitation can show up faster.

coachTitles.follow_ups: In follow-up, clarity is stronger than pressure
coachTitles.opportunities: Make the next step easier for the warm prospect
coachTitles.new_reachouts: Let the first touch feel safe and natural

coachSummaries.follow_ups: Reference the last conversation, ask one simple question, and leave the dialogue open. Light re-entry usually works better than long explanations.
coachSummaries.opportunities: Do not drown a warm person in detail. Confirm the interest, lower decision friction, and make the next step obvious.
coachSummaries.new_reachouts: A short curiosity-driven opening is more valuable than explaining the product or opportunity too early. Start a natural dialogue first.

dashboard.ctaText: Start adding your first contacts to use Network Marketing Master. Grow your team with AI-powered tools.

analytics.subtitle: Track your activity and contact distribution

signalBoard.title: Decision Signals
signalBoard.subtitle: See the risk, growth opportunity, and rhythm hiding underneath the numbers at a glance.

nextMove.risk: Start by cleaning up overdue follow-ups and warm people who have gone quiet. Do not push growth before the risk is under control.
nextMove.growth: Today you have room to move warm and fresh opportunities forward. Clear and low-friction next steps should lead the day.
nextMove.rhythm: Rhythm coverage looks soft. Instead of trying to touch everyone, add small but regular touches to the people who have gone less visible.
nextMove.balanced: The picture looks balanced. You can keep warm opportunities and daily follow-up moving together without building extra pressure.

risk.title: Risk Zone
risk.body: {{overdue}} overdue follow-ups and {{stalled}} warm-but-stalled people need attention right now.

growth.title: Growth Window
growth.body: {{ready}} people look warm enough for progress, and {{fresh}} people are well positioned for a fresh-touch rhythm.

rhythm.title: Rhythm Score
rhythm.body: About {{score}}% of your active contacts currently have visible follow-up coverage or a recent touch.

nextMove.profile: Keeping your profile clear helps the product feel more personal everywhere.
nextMove.settings: Once language and base preferences are in place, daily use feels much more natural.
nextMove.notifications: Turning on notifications helps keep follow-up and appointment rhythm visible.
nextMove.support: Going to the right support door inside the product saves momentum when you get stuck.
nextMove.feedback: Short feedback notes are one of the fastest ways to make the product more field-ready.
nextMove.account: This is your final checkpoint for sign-out and account safety.

support.description: Use the right support door for the job. Training, objections, and follow-up rhythm are usually the fastest ways to unblock field work.

academy.title: Academy Support
academy.body: Go to the training area to refresh your core explanation, team rhythm, and field execution.

objections.title: Objection Bank
objections.body: Open the objection flow for money, trust, time, or “I need to think about it” moments.

followUps.title: Follow-up Rhythm
followUps.body: Clean up overdue or due-today actions and get your rhythm visible again.

feedback.description: Capture what feels slow, what feels strong, and what would make the product simpler in the field. You can start from a ready-made template.
feedback.placeholder: For example: The Messages screen could surface quick filters more clearly. The new signal area in Pipeline really helps me decide faster.
feedback.template:
What did I try?

Where did I feel friction?

What clearly helped me move faster?

What could be simpler?

Which screen do I use most in the field?

stabilize.title: Stabilize First
stabilize.body: {{count}} overdue follow-ups are now at risk of cooling the conversation.

prepare.body: There are {{tomorrow}} follow-ups tomorrow and {{week}} more coming later this week.

nextMove.stabilize: Start by clearing overdue items. Adding new load before quiet conversations are stabilized can hurt rhythm further.
nextMove.deliver: Make today’s actions visible and complete. Short, clear follow-ups with a real next step will lighten the day.
nextMove.prepare: While the load is lighter, prepare tomorrow and the rest of the week. This is a good moment to simplify titles and next steps.
nextMove.steady: The follow-up load looks balanced. Keep the flow steady and make completed touches visible. Current coverage is about {{coverage}}%.

reconnect.body: {{count}} follow-ups are waiting for a response today. A low-pressure but clear re-entry message will help most here.
reconnect.reason: Today the priority is safely re-entering quiet or delayed conversations.

invite.body: {{count}} people look ready for a first approach or a soft invitation. Use short messages that spark curiosity.

decision.reason: Today it is more valuable to reduce friction for warm but hesitant people without adding pressure.

inviteFlow.title: Turn a new contact into an invite
inviteFlow.desc: Open the right content for first approach, curiosity, and low-pressure invitation language.

postPresentation.title: Protect post-presentation momentum
postPresentation.desc: Move into closing and follow-up content that keeps things clear without amplifying hesitation.

teamRhythm.title: Strengthen team rhythm
teamRhythm.desc: Open team-building content that helps new members stay in motion and keeps coaching simpler.

objections.title: Turn objections into dialogue
objections.desc: Move into the objection bank for ready answers, example dialogs, and low-pressure pivots.

scenarioCards.label: Field Scenarios

followUp.title: Re-open a quiet conversation
followUp.body: Open follow-up and closing content when timing is due but energy has dropped.

firstInvite.title: Turn the first message into an invite
firstInvite.body: Open the right flow for first approach, curiosity, and low-pressure invitation language.

postPresentation.title: Handle post-presentation hesitation
postPresentation.body: Move into closing and follow-up content that advances the next step without pressure.

teamActivation.title: Keep a new team member in motion
teamActivation.body: Open team-building and leadership content that helps a starter protect rhythm.

objection.subtitle: Ready, effective responses to the most common objections
objection.emptyDescription: You can create and keep your own objection responses here.

psychology.label: Objection Psychology
psychology.summary: An objection is usually not a final no; it is often a signal of risk, confusion, or identity protection. Lower the threat first, answer second, and leave a tiny next step last.

validate.title: Hear the emotion first
validate.body: People look for safety before logic. Before defending your idea, acknowledge why the concern could feel reasonable.

depressure.title: Reduce pressure
depressure.body: Do not speed up when an objection appears. Lower the threat level first. The goal is not to force agreement, but to make thinking feel safe.

microStep.title: Leave a micro next step
microStep.body: Do not end with a long answer. A small question, short resource, or light confirmation keeps the conversation alive.

quickScenarios.label: Quick Scenarios
quickScenarios.subtitle: Narrow the bank quickly based on the most common field hesitations.

money.title: Budget hesitation
money.body: Start here when product or business conversations are running into money resistance.

trust.title: Trust question
trust.body: Open this cluster when the person is holding back from the company, product, or system.

wait.title: I need to think
wait.body: See the replies that separate real thinking from quiet decision avoidance.

time.title: No time
time.body: Use this when the person feels overloaded and needs a lower-friction frame.

team.title: Team
team.subtitle: Track distributor contacts in one place, spot who needs support early, and keep momentum moving without losing visibility.

stats.activeRateHint: {{count}} people are currently in a warm flow
stats.newThisMonthHint: Team candidates added in the last 30 days
stats.joinedHint: {{count}} people are waiting for close follow-up

overview.title: Team Pulse
overview.body: %{{activeRate}} of your {{members}} team members stayed active recently. Catching quiet members early protects growth rhythm.
overview.followUpBody: {{count}} people already passed their follow-up date. This list is ready for your daily coaching flow.
overview.momentumBody: {{joined}} people already look joined. {{newThisMonth}} new team candidates entered the system in the last 30 days.

statusDescriptions.gaining_momentum: With the right support, this person could show visible movement this week.
statusDescriptions.active: Rhythm looks healthy; small check-ins may be enough.
statusDescriptions.slowing_down: A clear next step should be discussed before the connection fades.
statusDescriptions.needs_support: Silence has stretched or follow-up was missed; a short reset conversation is needed.

focus.newConversations: Building a rhythm of new conversations
focus.presentationSupport: Turning post-presentation energy into the right next move
focus.decisionSupport: Reducing pressure near the decision stage

suggestions.praiseAndExpand: Give short recognition and help expand this momentum into one clear next move.
suggestions.lightCheckIn: A brief encouragement check-in with simple direction is usually enough.
suggestions.defineNextStep: Reduce ambiguity. Define one clear follow-up move for this week.
suggestions.resetWithOneGoal: Do not overload them. Help them focus on just one small target for this week.

momentum.building: Warmth and rhythm are rising together.
momentum.stable: The relationship is open and the rhythm is holding.
momentum.fragile: There is momentum, but it can easily scatter.
momentum.recovery: First re-open contact and create a safe base again.

reasons.overdue: Follow-up date already passed, so it's worth returning before warmth drops.
reasons.noRecentTouch: There hasn't been recent contact, so refreshing the connection could help.
reasons.noTouchYet: A regular contact rhythm has not been established yet.

empty.attention: No critical support case is waiting right now.
empty.activation: No team member is currently waiting in the activation queue.

focus.title: Today's Coaching Focus
focus.body: Start with the {{count}} people who need support, then move the warm names in the activation queue forward. That order usually keeps team momentum steadier.

hero.subtitle: Manage contacts, generate AI messages, grow your team — all in one place. Scattered lists and missed follow-ups are history.

panel.label: Live Operating Flow
panel.title: Not just a CRM, a daily working system
panel.subtitle: Turn scattered lead lists into a rhythm that returns to the right person at the right time.
panel.assistantAction: The system suggests closing the overdue follow-up first, then moving the warm opportunity.
panel.assistantMessage: Start with the person who went quiet but still carries warmth. Then offer one clear next move to the opportunity near decision.

tasks.title: Overdue follow-up
tasks.meta: A conversation that needed your reply yesterday is still warm today.

tasks.title: Post-presentation window

tasks.title: Fresh reach-out list
tasks.meta: New people are waiting for a natural first touch.

operations.desc: Contacts, follow-ups, messaging, team, and academy move in the same rhythm.

priorities.title: Not guesswork, but priority order
priorities.desc: The system makes visible which conversation deserves attention first today.

guidance.title: Guidance aligned with distributor psychology
guidance.desc: Objections, follow-ups, and team leadership move with less pressure and more clarity.

items.title: Scattered lists, lost follow-ups
items.desc: Notebooks, WhatsApp, Excel... Contacts are everywhere but nowhere organized.

items.title: "I'll think about it" — then what?
items.desc: You presented, they showed interest. But when will you follow up? How many days later?

items.title: Don't know who's active in the team
items.desc: As your downline grows, tracking becomes harder. Who needs support? You don't know.

features.title: Everything in one platform
features.subtitle: All the tools you need for network marketing success

items.title: Smart Daily Task List
items.desc: AI tells you who to reach out to today. No guessing, just prioritized actions.

items.title: Lead Warmth Score
items.desc: Track each contact's readiness level in real-time. Reach the right person at the right time.

items.title: Pipeline Tracking
items.desc: Visually track every stage of your sales process. Never miss an opportunity.

items.title: Team Radar

items.title: Objection Bank

steps.title: Reach the Right Person

items.q: What is NMM and who is it for?
items.a: Network Marketing Master (NMM) is a digital management tool for multi-level marketing (MLM) distributors. It offers contact tracking, AI-powered messaging, and team management in one platform.
Yes, to get started! You can begin with 50 contacts and basic features. As your team grows, upgrading to Pro or Team is easy.
Yes. All data is stored encrypted on Supabase (PostgreSQL). With Row Level Security, only you can access your data.

items.q: Which network marketing companies does it work with?
items.a: NMM is company-independent. It can be used with Amway, Herbalife, Oriflame, Avon and all MLM companies.
The web app works optimized on all mobile devices. Native iOS/Android apps are on our roadmap.
Yes, you can cancel anytime. Billing is on a monthly basis, cancellation takes effect at the end of the billing period.

cta.title: Start today, make a difference
cta.subtitle: No credit card required. 2-minute setup. Cancel anytime.
